title: helianthemum pilosum subsp. violaceum
intro: Helianthemum pilosum subsp. violaceum, also known as Purple Rockrose, is a flowering plant species in the family Cistaceae. It is a perennial shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula, known for its fragrant, pale purple flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as inflammation, wounds, and respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Helianthemum pilosum subsp. violaceum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
